title: Code Points, Grapheme Clusters, Character Categories, and Labels · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for code points, grapheme clusters, character categories, and labels and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, terminal, packaging, operating-system, load, security, and release transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for honestly labeled text-metrics panel. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggi…
objectives: Distinguish bytes, code points, extended grapheme clusters, display cells, letters, and stakeholder-defined characters.
Use Unicode categories or explicit predicates to separate letters, marks, numbers, punctuation, symbols, separators, and controls.
State whether character frequencies operate on original or normalized text, preserve case, and include or exclude marks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Metric Label Evidence
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 8824 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to label and reconcile code-point, grapheme, and letter metrics without overstating browser segmentation; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Label every metric with unit, inclusion policy, input version, and reconciliation or non-reconciliation rule.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Metric Label Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Metric Label Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 8633 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to label and reconcile code-point, grapheme, and letter metrics without overstating browser segmentation; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Label every metric with unit, inclusion policy, input version, and reconciliation or non-reconciliation rule.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Metric Label Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Metric Label Evidence through the concrete honestly labeled text-metrics panel case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Metric Label Evidence, keep Metric Label Evidence connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Label every metric with unit, inclusion policy, input version, and reconciliation or non-reconciliation rule.
